task:
okay now that we have added .lightfast root repo.

we want to basically design a few new features.

1. skills, users basically will write skills in a folder in .lightfast called .skills/....., as such let's adopt cluade code's approach to how skills are structured.
2. let's then add a post-processing after entities are ingested.


consider these few scenarios:
1. we made changes in core/lightfast, technically, in this sense, if we had a skill that was basically like "yo when this changes, we should tell the user to create a npm publish + updates docs". this is the idea of triaging using skills.
2. a sentry errors comes in, super crtical, it's in prod, send a slack message to lightfast discord.

moreover a second feature:
1. it knows what user is currently building.


right now the .lightfast is pretty bare. but consider this, we allow users to design a more complex structure for .lightfast, a general rule of thumb: we adopt SPEC.md as the root explainer for what the company is.

so basically this ai agent runs with 

some internal system prompt + spec.md + skills (adopted from claude's approach to skills) 

and it has a bunch of TOOLS to do things. e.g it has access to @apps/app/src/app/(api)/v1/answer/[...v]/route.ts 's tools.
why is our skill format so complex? why not just let the agent decided? all skills loaded all the time.
user_feedback_text: why is our skill format so complex? why not just let the agent decided? all skills loaded all the time.
